attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Set two statues on the table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a living room environment with a central wooden coffee table placed on a patterned area rug. On the coffee table, there are two small, dark-colored statues, each standing on a separate square base. A remote control with colorful buttons is also on the table, positioned between the two statues. Additionally, there is a small, circular, dark object (possibly a coaster or a ring) near the remote.

In the background, a red sofa is visible. On the sofa, there is an open laptop and a cardboard box. The laptop is positioned on the left cushion, and the box is on the right cushion.

The domain defines types such as `agent`, `receptacle`, and `object`. In this scene, the coffee table and the sofa can be considered receptacles since they can hold objects. The statues, remote control, laptop, and box are objects. The statues are currently on the coffee table, which is a receptacle.

The robot (agent) is not visible in the scene, and there is no indication of its current location. The instruction is to set two statues on the table, which is already accomplished as both statues are on the coffee table.

The coffee table is made of dark wood and has a rectangular shape. The statues are dark in color, possibly black or dark bronze, and are positioned symmetrically on the table. The remote control is light-colored with colored buttons. The sofa is red with a textured fabric. The laptop is silver or gray, and the box is a standard cardboard color.

There are no openable receptacles in the scene that are relevant to the instruction. The statues are not being held by any agent, and there is no indication that they have been cleaned, heated, cooled, toggled, or sliced. The current state of the objects relevant to the planning task is that the two statues are already placed on the coffee table, fulfilling the instruction.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem set_statues_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        statue1 statue2 remote_control - object
        coffee_table sofa - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle remote_control coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem set_statues_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        statue1 statue2 remote_control - object
        coffee_table sofa - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle remote_control coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table)
        (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table)
    ))
)

Scene Description:

The scene depicts a living room environment with a central wooden coffee table placed on a patterned area rug. On the coffee table, there are two small, dark-colored statues, each standing on a separate square base. These statues are the primary objects of interest for the given instruction. Also on the table is a remote control with colorful buttons and a small, circular object that appears to be a coaster or a ring.

In the background, there is a red sofa with a laptop and an open cardboard box resting on it. To the left, a portion of a light-colored armchair is visible. A second, smaller black end table is positioned near the wall on the right side of the room, but it is currently empty.

The two statues are not in any receptacle and are directly placed on the surface of the coffee table. According to the domain, the coffee table is an object (not a receptacle) since it is not defined as a microwave, fridge, or sink. Therefore, the statues are simply “on” the table, not “in” a receptacle. The statues are not held by any agent and are not currently being manipulated.

The robot agent is not visible in the scene and is initially not at any object’s location. To perform the task of setting the statues on the table, the robot must first navigate to the table (GotoLocation) and then interact with the statues if they are not already in the desired position. However, since the statues are already on the table, the task may be considered complete if the goal is simply to have them placed there.

No objects in the scene are currently held by an agent, and no receptacles (microwave, fridge, sink) are visible or relevant to this specific instruction. The statues are not clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled, as these predicates do not apply to them in this context.

In summary, the scene contains:
- Two dark-colored statues on square bases, positioned on a wooden coffee table.
- One remote control and one small circular object also on the table.
- The coffee table is on a patterned rug, with a red sofa and an end table in the surrounding area.
- No agent is present, and no objects are being held or manipulated.
- The statues are already on the table, satisfying the instruction to “set two statues on the table.”

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem set_statues_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        statue1 statue2 - object
        coffee_table - object
        remote_control coaster - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 statue1))
        (not (holds agent1 statue2))
        (not (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table))
        (not (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (atLocation agent1 coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 statue1))
        (not (holds agent1 statue2))
        (not (inReceptacle statue1 coffee_table))
        (not (inReceptacle statue2 coffee_table))
```

You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
